title: 9 4 Fourier series with Arbitrary period
body:
9.4 FOURIER SERIES OVER AN ARBITRARY PERIOD

A periodic function f with period T = 2L can be approximated by the Fourier series ∞ X nπx nπx f (x) = a0 + (an cos + bn sin ) n=1 L L

where the Fourier coefficients a0 , an , and bn are given by

RL a0 = 2L f (x) dx −L

RL nπx an = L1 f (x) cos dx (n = 1, 2, ) −L L

RL nπx bn = L1 f (x) sin dx (n = 1, 2, ) −L L ∞ P nπx • Odd functions have odd series f (x) = bn sin n=1 L ∞ P nπx • Even functions have even series f (x) = a0 + an cos n=1 L

We turn now to the Fourier series of function with arbitrary period T . To simplify the equations we define L to be half the period so that T = 2L and express the equations in terms of L.

Observe from the equations above that if we replace L by π (and hence T by 2π) we obtain exactly the equations which we have been using over the last few lectures. The formulae above will be supplied to you in your final examination and they are all that you need to find all the different types of Fourier series.   | x| −3 ≤ x < 3; Example 1 Let f (x) = f (x + 6) otherwise. 

i) Sketch f over −9 ≤ x < 9. ii) Find the Fourier series of f . iii) Express the Fourier series of f as a sum over the odd integers. iv) By considering the series at x = 0 show that

  x −1 ≤ x < 1; Example 2 Let f (x) = f (x + 2) otherwise. 

i) Sketch f over −3 ≤ x < 3. ii) Find the Fourier series of f . iii) To what value does the Fourier series converge at x = 1? iv) By considering the series at x = show that π 1 1 1 = 1 − + − + ... 4 3 5 7

∞ 2 (−1)n+1 sin(nπx) P F f (x) = Converges to 0 at x = 1 F n=1 nπ Notice that the original function f (x) was discontinuous at the point x = 1 but its Fourier series is continuous at this point. That’s because Fourier series are made up of sines and cosines which are continuous functions. More generally, if the function f (x) is discontinuous at the point x = a then its Fourier series will converge to the average value across the discontinuity f (a− ) + f (a+ )  f (x) → You can now do Q 110
